label: vulnerable

source.description: User-controlled URL string from query parameter 'url' received in the route layer. No scheme, host, or format check is applied.

propagation.description: url is forwarded unchanged to ProxyService.fetch_remote() — no validation at the route-to-service boundary.
ProxyService.fetch_remote() passes url directly to get_url() without any sanitization — no validation at the service-to-utils boundary.

sink.description: requests.get() initiates an outbound HTTP request to the attacker-controlled URL. Internal addresses such as http://169.254.169.254/ are reachable.

expected_findings.risk_reasoning: The url value propagates through three layers (route → service → utils) without any validation. It enters requests.get() in the utils layer. An attacker can target cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), internal services, or arbitrary internet hosts.
expected_findings.remediation_hint: Validate the URL in the service layer before delegating to utils: enforce https-only scheme, restrict hostnames to a domain allowlist, and block private/loopback/link-local IP ranges. Pass allow_redirects=False to requests.get().

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the sink in utils/http_utils.py. Source identification in routes/proxy_routes.py is additional credit.

notes: Cross-file SSRF taint tracing: route layer (source) → service layer (passthrough) → utils layer (sink). Each boundary is an opportunity for a system to lose track of the taint.
